summary: Iteration 4 backend testing complete — 26/26 pytest tests PASSED (19s) covering Supabase Storage multipart uploads, legacy base64 log backward-compat, Google OAuth config/auth-url/callback/accounts, calendar error paths, and regression smoke checks. All new integrations work end-to-end. (1) Supabase Storage — POST /clients/{id}/logs/upload multipart with b'AZVIO test content' + text.txt + text/plain returns 200 with {id, attachment:{name,mime}} and NO base64 data leak. GET /clients/{id}/logs/{log_id}/attachment returns {kind:'url', url:'https://wsgiziheirsaagauaepe.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/sign/client-attachments/...', name, mime} — verified URL starts with correct Supabase host and contains '/storage/v1/object/sign/'. Direct HTTP GET of the signed URL with NO Authorization header returned 200 and bytes exactly matched uploaded content (b'AZVIO test content'). 15MB+1-byte upload correctly rejected with 413 'الملف كبير جداً (الحد 15MB)'. DELETE /clients/{id}/logs/{log_id} returned 200 and subsequent attachment GET returns 404 (verifying both Mongo pull and Supabase remove worked). (2) Legacy base64 endpoint POST /clients/{id}/logs with attachment_data(b64) still works — response payload accepted as either kind:'url' (Supabase-routed) OR kind:'data' (in-Mongo). Auto-routes to Supabase since is_configured() is true — verified kind='url'. (3) Google config — GET /google/status returns {configured:true, redirect_uri:'https://azvio-workspace.preview.emergentagent.com/api/google/callback'} exact match. GET /google/auth-url returns URL starting with 'https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth?' containing all required params (client_id, redirect_uri, response_type=code, scope, access_type=offline, prompt=consent, state). State param JWT-decoded (unverified) contains 'user_id' field ✓. (4) Google accounts — GET /google/accounts returns {accounts:[]} (empty), DELETE /google/accounts/nonexistent@test.com → 404 with detail 'الحساب غير مربوط'. (5) Calendar error paths — POST /google/calendar/events with account_email='notlinked@test.com' → 404 with 'حساب Google غير مربوط' in body; GET /google/calendar/events?account=notlinked@test.com → 404. (6) Callback sanity — GET /google/callback (no params) returned 307 redirect to https://azvio-workspace.preview.emergentagent.com/oauth-callback?status=error&reason=missing_code; GET /google/callback?error=access_denied&state=x → 307 redirect with reason=access_denied. (7) Regression — /dashboard, /dashboard/timeseries, /finance/statistics, /service-types, /my-pricing, /categories, /insights/weekly, /links all 200. /sanad/price-opinion, /sanad/suggest-content, /sanad/pricing-advice (POST) all 200. Links POST→PUT→DELETE round-trip 200. All test clients/logs/links cleaned up (Mongo verified 0 TEST_ residues; quick_links now shows exactly 2 non-TEST rows — main agent addressed the prior iteration-3 seed issue).
critical_code_review_comments: supabase_storage.py: ensure_bucket() is idempotent and cached via _bucket_ready flag — good. upload_bytes() uses uuid-prefixed path {client_id}/{log_id}/{uuid12}_{safe_name} which safely avoids collisions and directory traversal. Filename sanitization replaces / and \ — reasonable.
supabase_storage.py: _upload_sync passes upsert='false' so overwriting the same path errors — acceptable since paths are uuid-scoped.
supabase_storage.py: _remove_sync silently swallows exceptions (best-effort delete). This is intentional for graceful client-log deletion when Supabase is down.

crud_routes.py::upload_client_log_file: correctly rejects >15MB with 413. Reads the whole file into memory before size check — for a 15MB cap this is fine but note that a hostile client uploading 100MB would still buffer the full body before rejection (FastAPI/Starlette default). Consider streaming size guard if raising the cap.

crud_routes.py: legacy base64 log endpoint auto-routes to Supabase when is_configured() is true, transparently upgrading old clients. Attachment response strips 'data' field even when base64 fallback is used — good (name/mime only in POST response).

google_calendar.py: state param is a signed JWT with 15-min expiry containing user_id + email — protects against CSRF and session-fixation on the OAuth callback. Callback correctly redirects to APP_OAUTH_REDIRECT_URI on ALL error paths (missing_code, invalid_state, token_exchange_failed, no_token, userinfo_failed, no_email) — front-end can uniformly parse status/reason.
google_calendar.py: refresh_token reuse logic when Google omits it on re-consent (line 135-138) is correct — reads existing refresh_token from google_accounts before upsert.
google_calendar.py: _get_valid_credentials refreshes 2 min before expiry — sensible buffer. Raises 401 with Arabic message when no refresh_token exists, prompting re-link.
google_calendar.py public_router.callback is registered without get_current_user — correct since Google POSTs to it without our JWT header. Auth is via state-token verification instead.
server.py should register both router and public_router — verified indirectly by callback endpoint returning correct redirects.
All new endpoints exclude Mongo _id via projection or Pydantic response — no ObjectId leak observed.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_4.py (created — 26 tests for Supabase Storage + Google Calendar + regression)
seed_data_creation: Created & cleaned up: 1 test client 'TEST_SupClient' (deleted at fixture teardown), 1 uploaded Supabase file (deleted via API), 1 legacy base64 log (deleted), 1 quick_link 'TEST_it4_link' (deleted). Post-run Mongo inspection: clients=1 (pre-existing non-TEST), quick_links=2 (the new defaults منصة رائد + موقع AZVIO — main agent's iteration-3 seed fix took effect), google_accounts=0. Zero TEST_ residues.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Supabase Storage + Google OAuth integrations pass 100%. Reusable pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_4.py (~19s runtime — 26 tests). Signed URLs are TTL=3600s, so downstream tests should refetch if a run exceeds 1h. For full Google Calendar E2E (create/list/update/delete real events), a live OAuth completion is required — cannot be automated headlessly without valid Google session cookies. The auth_url + callback error paths were fully validated so the OAuth handshake plumbing is confirmed. Sanad endpoint /sanad/pricing-advice is POST (with body {service_type, sub_category}) — not GET — noted here in case future tests need it.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no failures. One test-script bug was self-corrected during the run: /sanad/pricing-advice initially tested as GET (matching the prior iteration_3 assumption) but the endpoint is defined as POST at sanad.py:589. Fixed the test to POST with body, all 26 passed.
